records.ety: [OE. slautir, slaughter, slaghter, Icel. slātr slain flesh, modified by OE. slaught, slaht, slaughter, fr. AS. sleaht a stroke, blow; both from the root of E. slay. See Slay, v. t., and cf. Onslaught.]

defs: The extensive, violent, bloody, or wanton destruction of life; carnage.
The act of killing cattle or other beasts for market.
quotes: On war and mutual slaughter bent.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Slaughtered (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Slaughtering.]

defs: To visit with great destruction of life; to kill; to slay in battle.
quotes: Your castle is surprised; your wife and babes Savagely slaughtered.

defs: To butcher; to kill for the market, as beasts.
